description:
You are a backup-operator who needs to verify three nights of restore tests.  
  The following plain–text log files already exist:

    /home/user/backup_logs/restore_20240601.log  
    /home/user/backup_logs/restore_20240602.log  
    /home/user/backup_logs/restore_20240603.log  

  Every file contains one or more logical “restore transactions”.  Each
  transaction always consists of exactly two lines:

    • a “START” line  
         YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS START <path> size=<bytes>  
    • a matching “END” line  
         YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS END   <path> duration=<seconds>s status=<SUCCESS|FAILURE> [code=<err-code>]

  Notes and requirements:

  1. All byte values are unsigned integers written in decimal.  
  2. All duration values end with the character “s”.  
  3. If status is FAILURE, the field “code=<ERR>” is **always** present.

  Your tasks

  A. Create the directory /home/user/restore_reports/ (it does not exist yet).  
  B. Produce one consolidated CSV file named  
        /home/user/restore_reports/restore_summary.csv  
     The CSV **must** use a single header row followed by one row per log file, **sorted by date ascending**.  
     Field order, spelling and capitalisation must be *exactly*:

        date,total_restores,successful,failed,total_bytes,avg_duration_seconds

     − date                       : yyyymmdd (no dashes) corresponding to the log-file date.  
     − total_restores             : total number of restore transactions in that log.  
     − successful                 : count of transactions whose END line has status=SUCCESS.  
     − failed                     : count of transactions whose END line has status=FAILURE.  
     − total_bytes                : sum of the “size=” values of all START lines.  
     − avg_duration_seconds       : arithmetic mean of all “duration=” values, rounded
                                    to the nearest whole second (standard 0.5 → round-up).

     The automated test will reject  
       • wrong header names,  
       • additional / missing columns,  
       • date order errors,  
       • any value that is not an integer (except the header).

  C. Extract every failed transaction and write them—one per line—into  

        /home/user/restore_reports/failed_restore_log.txt  

     The required format (tab-separated!) for each line is:

        YYYY-MM-DD<TAB><path><TAB><error_code>

     1. YYYY-MM-DD must match the date on the END line.  
     2. <path> is the absolute path that failed.  
     3. <error_code> is the value that appeared after “code=”.

     The test harness will check that:  
       • only failed restores are present,  
       • lines are ordered by the date then by appearance in the source file,  
       • the file is tab-delimited (not spaces).

  D. Make sure both /home/user/restore_reports/restore_summary.csv and
     /home/user/restore_reports/failed_restore_log.txt are readable (`-rw-r--r--` or more permissive).

  DO NOT hard-code the numbers; derive them by parsing the supplied logs.
  Display the resulting files to the console so the reviewer can see your work.
truth:
<!--
    Pre-existing files
  -->
  <file path="/home/user/backup_logs/restore_20240601.log"><![CDATA[
2024-06-01 00:00:01 START /srv/data size=15728640
2024-06-01 00:05:02 END   /srv/data duration=301s status=SUCCESS
2024-06-01 01:10:00 START /srv/media size=31457280
2024-06-01 01:30:31 END   /srv/media duration=1231s status=FAILURE code=E42
]]></file>

  <file path="/home/user/backup_logs/restore_20240602.log"><![CDATA[
2024-06-02 02:00:00 START /srv/data size=15728640
2024-06-02 02:05:03 END   /srv/data duration=303s status=SUCCESS
2024-06-02 03:15:10 START /srv/docs size=1048576
2024-06-02 03:18:00 END   /srv/docs duration=170s status=SUCCESS
2024-06-02 04:00:00 START /srv/media size=41943040
2024-06-02 04:30:05 END   /srv/media duration=1805s status=SUCCESS
]]></file>

  <file path="/home/user/backup_logs/restore_20240603.log"><![CDATA[
2024-06-03 00:00:05 START /srv/data size=15728640
2024-06-03 00:05:10 END   /srv/data duration=305s status=FAILURE code=E13
2024-06-03 01:20:00 START /srv/media size=10485760
2024-06-03 01:22:10 END   /srv/media duration=130s status=SUCCESS
]]></file>

  <!--
    Expected artefacts created by the agent
  -->
  <expected_file path="/home/user/restore_reports/restore_summary.csv"><![CDATA[
date,total_restores,successful,failed,total_bytes,avg_duration_seconds
20240601,2,1,1,47185920,766
20240602,3,3,0,58720256,759
20240603,2,1,1,26214400,218
]]></expected_file>

  <expected_file path="/home/user/restore_reports/failed_restore_log.txt"><![CDATA[
2024-06-01	/srv/media	E42
2024-06-03	/srv/data	E13
]]></expected_file>
